sourceEvidence.label: Pelvic Ring Injury — Learning objectives
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Learning objectives for Pelvic Ring Injury

objectives: Recognize unstable pelvic ring patterns and hemorrhage risk.
Apply Young-Burgess mechanism language to injury severity.
Prioritize binder, resuscitation, and stabilization before definitive fixation.

mustKnow: Mechanism, ring stability, binder, hemorrhage language, resuscitation priority
payload.oneLiner: Pelvic ring injury is a resuscitation problem first — instability and hemorrhage drive urgency before definitive fixation.
pimpQuestions: What makes a pelvic ring unstable?
When is a pelvic binder indicated?
orSurvivalTips: Mention binder and ex-fix as temporizing tools

metadata.claim_text: State mechanism, hemodynamic status, binder placement, ring stability assessment, transfusion needs, and ex-fix versus definitive OR plan.

metadata.action: Apply pelvic binder if not in place; discuss anterior external fixation or definitive stabilization with trauma team and attending
metadata.trigger: Unstable pelvic ring pattern with mechanical instability after resuscitation

metadata.description: Life-threatening retroperitoneal bleeding from pelvic ring and venous plexus injury.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Unstable Pelvic Ring Pattern

metadata.description: Radiographic pattern with anterior and/or posterior ring disruption indicating mechanical instability.
Emergency circumferential compression to reduce pelvic volume and tamponade hemorrhage.
High-energy pelvic ring disruption with instability, hemorrhage, and resuscitation priorities.

metadata.claim_text: Pelvic ring injuries are evaluated as anterior and posterior ring disruptions where instability pattern drives binder, ex-fix, and hemorrhage control priorities.
Pelvic binder application temporizes hemorrhage but does not replace assessment of unstable ring patterns requiring definitive stabilization.
Sacroiliac joint and pubic symphysis integrity define anterior versus posterior pelvic ring stability contributions.
Focusing only on pubic symphysis widening without evaluating sacroiliac disruption in high-energy pelvic trauma.

metadata.action: Activate massive transfusion pathway, pelvic binder, angioembolization discussion, and expedited ring stabilization per attending
metadata.trigger: Hemodynamically unstable patient with pelvic ring injury and suspected pelvic hemorrhage

metadata.description: Mechanism-based pelvic ring injury classification (APC, LC, VS).
Anterior frame stabilization for unstable pelvic ring injuries during resuscitation or definitive care.
